source_name: NREL / kWh Analytics / Verisk — Photovoltaic (PV) Insurance Claims analysis (Technical Report NREL/TP-5C00-78588)
solar_flood_use: Only open PV-specific claims dataset with a Flood/Water peril group; gives per-claim severity and count context for water-related solar damage.
has_open_numbers: 15,128 claims 2014–2019; $60.8M solar item value; >$1.2B total insured value in study. Flood/Water group reported per-claim statistics, but the published table has a column-alignment ambiguity: reading A avg $2,020 / median $1,210 / max $26,103 / stdev $2,839 (n=197); reading B avg $5,288 / median $2,358 / max $94,921 / stdev $12,143 (n=79).
does_not_have_open: Per-MW normalization, project TIV, capacity per claim, unambiguous flood column; original PDF table must be visually re-checked before either reading is trusted.
notes: Both column readings must be carried forward and flagged; do not silently pick one. Most concrete open PV flood-claim severity source found.

source_name: BMS Group / GCube — Australian renewables claims review
solar_flood_use: Peril-mix share for solar where NatCat (including flood) is broken out relative to wind and contractor error.
has_open_numbers: NatCat (incl. flood) ≈ 22% of solar claims quantum vs ≈ 10% for wind; contractor error ≈ 68% of solar claims; solar external-peril share ≈ 72%.
does_not_have_open: Flood-only isolation from the NatCat bucket; $/MW; TIV; per-project denominators.
notes: NatCat bucket bundles flood with other perils; usable only as peril-share context, not a flood-only benchmark.

source_name: GCube — Arrested Development (construction-phase renewables claims report)
solar_flood_use: Construction-phase (CAR/DSU) flood frequency-vs-severity shape for wind+solar combined.
has_open_numbers: Flooding / heavy rainfall = 18% of weather-related construction claims by frequency but 46% by cost (≈ 2.6x severity multiplier); >$1B combined CAR + DSU claims in scope.
does_not_have_open: Solar-only split (wind+solar combined); $/MW; operational-phase applicability; TIV.
notes: Strong flood severity-multiplier signal, but construction-phase and mixed-technology; label accordingly.

source_name: AXIS / HAEE — legacy renewable energy severity bucket guidance
solar_flood_use: Qualitative severity-bucket scale and a named solar CBI flood-adjacent loss for order-of-magnitude context.
has_open_numbers: Severity buckets cited at USD 250k / 1M / 5M / 10M+; Puerto Rico solar contingent business interruption loss ≈ $20M.
does_not_have_open: Per-MW normalization; flood-only isolation; project TIV; distribution.
notes: Legacy qualitative buckets only; CBI figure is order-of-magnitude, not a normalized benchmark.

source_name: Swiss Re — Catching the Sun (solar PV NatCat claims study)
solar_flood_use: Confirms peril hierarchy for solar PV but does not break out a flood share in the open material.
has_open_numbers: Hail cited as 3% of claims but ≈ 60% of losses; no open flood-specific percentage.
does_not_have_open: Any flood-only claim share or $/MW; flood is not separately quantified in open excerpts.
notes: Negative for flood in the open material; treat as pathway/screening source for a fuller gated study.

source_name: Marsh — renewable energy property claims context (storm/hail dominance)
solar_flood_use: Bundled storm-peril share that implicitly caps a residual flood share for solar PV claim values.
has_open_numbers: Storm + hail together ≈ 80% of solar PV claim values; flood falls in the residual not separately broken out.
does_not_have_open: Flood-only isolation; $/MW; TIV; denominators.
notes: Flood is inside the residual band; usable only to bound, not to quantify, flood share.

source_name: GCube — Hail or High Water (peril-dominance commentary)
solar_flood_use: Qualitative signal that for solar the dominant catastrophic peril is not flood (wildfire/hail lead), while wind portfolios are more flood-exposed.
has_open_numbers: Qualitative only: solar loss profile wildfire/hail-dominant; wind loss profile flood-dominant.
does_not_have_open: Any numeric flood share or severity for solar.
notes: Negative/contextual finding; explains why isolated solar flood $/MW is genuinely scarce.

source_name: Allianz Commercial — greening/renewables claims trends
solar_flood_use: Names solar flood loss events (Australia, Germany, UAE) as trend markers without dollar figures.
has_open_numbers: Named flood events affecting solar in AU / DE / UAE; no per-event or per-MW dollar figures.
does_not_have_open: Any dollar loss, capacity, or TIV.
notes: Pathway/trend evidence only; acquire underlying loss runs to quantify.

source_name: Moody's RMS — solar farm flood exposure disaggregation case
solar_flood_use: Only open modeled statement of how much flood AAL moves for a solar farm when exposure is properly disaggregated.
has_open_numbers: Flood AAL +230% vs windstorm +2% after exposure disaggregation on a 2,700-acre reservoir-adjacent site.
does_not_have_open: Absolute AAL dollars; $/MW; TIV; site identity; capacity.
notes: Relative delta only; strong evidence that flood AAL is highly geolocation-sensitive for solar. Absolute values gated.

source_name: Moody's RMS — U.S. Inland Flood HD model (industry-wide figures)
solar_flood_use: Industry-scale inland-flood loss and protection-gap context; not solar-specific but sets the hazard-model backdrop.
has_open_numbers: 1-in-100 U.S. inland flood ≈ $375B with ≈ 65% protection gap; +RCP4.5 2050 ≈ $472B; 1-in-500 > $1T with > 70% gap.
does_not_have_open: Solar-specific AAL/PML; asset-class breakouts; county solar bands.
notes: All-industry hazard model; provides scale, not a solar benchmark.

source_name: VDE Americas — All-NatCat risk & insurability report (inland flood component)
solar_flood_use: Cleanest gated path to site- and technology-specific solar flood AAL/PML alongside other perils.
has_open_numbers: Open pages confirm inland flood is included in the site-specific, lat/long, technology-specific NatCat product.
does_not_have_open: Actual flood AAL/PML values; requires commercial report.
notes: Best acquisition pathway for comparison-ready solar flood AAL/PML; not open-data.

source_name: Fathom — global flood hazard maps (used in solar site screening)
solar_flood_use: Hazard-depth layer used by developers/EDF-style screens to flag flood-unsuitable solar parcels.
has_open_numbers: Screening rule of thumb cited in solar siting: >3 ft modeled depth commonly treated as unsuitable / high-risk.
does_not_have_open: Dollar losses; AAL/PML; solar depth-damage functions.
notes: Hazard, not loss; feeds the depth axis of any damage function.

source_name: JBA Risk Management — flood model and depth-damage methodology
solar_flood_use: Documents the canonical flood-loss formula L = EV × DR(FD) and absolute-damage %sum-insured curves.
has_open_numbers: L = EV × DR(FD); absolute damage ratio expressed as % of sum insured; ≈ 38.6M of ≈ 130M U.S. buildings at flood risk (generic exposure).
does_not_have_open: Solar-specific DR curve; solar AAL/PML values.
notes: Provides the methodology (formula + curve shape), not solar numbers.

source_name: First Street Foundation — Cost of Climate / flood AAL model
solar_flood_use: Open property-level flood AAL magnitudes and climate uplift; a generic (non-solar) reference band for AAL scale.
has_open_numbers: 5.7M properties with substantial flood risk; avg AAL $3,548/yr; $20.3B/yr aggregate; +67% by 2050 to $5,913/yr avg; ≈ 2x USACE estimates.
does_not_have_open: Solar-specific losses; utility-scale asset curves; $/MW.
notes: Residential/commercial building AAL; use only as generic magnitude context, not a solar benchmark.

source_name: FEMA — Hazus Flood Technical Manual depth-damage functions
solar_flood_use: Generic-industrial depth-damage curve library that must proxy for the missing solar-specific curve.
has_open_numbers: 196 curves / 33 occupancy classes; haz_fl_dept coverage grid −4 to +24 ft; industrial/commercial occupancy curves usable as coarse solar-BOS proxy.
does_not_have_open: Any solar PV / BOS / inverter-specific curve.
notes: Generic curves only; label as generic-industrial proxy wherever used for solar.

source_name: USACE — depth-damage curves and freeboard AAL reduction guidance
solar_flood_use: Quantifies how elevation/freeboard cuts AAL — a mitigation-knob analog to hail stow for the flood pair.
has_open_numbers: No-freeboard max AAL 11.21% → +1 ft 2.002% (≈ 82% reduction); design inundation point −0.5→0 ft = 86.3% reduction; −2 ft → first-floor = 92% reduction.
does_not_have_open: Solar-specific curves; $/MW; solar AAL.
notes: Generic building curves; the freeboard reduction ratios are the transferable mitigation signal.

source_name: European Commission JRC — Global flood depth-damage functions (2017)
solar_flood_use: Generic mechanism-shape reference (North America Industrial depth-damage curve); NOT used on the crosswalk hazard axis (the canonical flood_solar per-failure-unit depth->DR curve from Damage_Modeling is used there).
does_not_have_open: Any solar PV / BOS-specific curve; damage-to-value calibration for solar.
notes: Retained only as generic mechanism-shape context; explicitly generic, not solar-calibrated. Superseded on the crosswalk hazard axis by the canonical flood_solar curve.

source_name: FM Global — Data Sheets 7-106 / 1-40 (solar & flood property protection)
solar_flood_use: Solar-specific engineering guidance identifying the flood failure unit (electrical/BOS) and elevation thresholds.
has_open_numbers: Elevate critical equipment 1–2 ft above the 500-yr flood MRI; erosion/scour risk noted above ≈ 7 ft/s flow velocity.
does_not_have_open: Dollar losses; AAL/PML; depth-damage percentages.
notes: Key source for the flood failure-unit argument (inverters/BOS/foundations, not module glass).

source_name: Renew Risk — solar cat-model (no dedicated flood peril)
solar_flood_use: Documents a gap: the leading dedicated solar SCS cat-model does not currently offer an inland-flood peril.
has_open_numbers: None for flood; product covers hail/tornado/wind for solar, flood not offered as of retrieval.
does_not_have_open: Any solar flood AAL/PML; a flood peril at all.
notes: Negative finding; reinforces scarcity of purpose-built solar flood modeling.

source_name: First Solar Electric v. Zurich American — flood coverage litigation
solar_flood_use: Most reliable open-dollar solar flood loss: asserted claim, per-event deductibles, amount paid, and depth are all in the record.
has_open_numbers: Asserted loss $10,146,341.29 (press: > $13M); per-event amounts $1,477,618 / $418,746 / $1,947,934 / $496,850 / $576,155 (≈ $4.92M); $600,000 paid ($200,000 × 3 events) under a $2.5M per-event flood deductible; > $141.9M value in place; ≈ 13 ft flood depth.
does_not_have_open: Project MW capacity; TIV as a clean denominator; ground-up vs net reconciliation.
notes: Deductible structure is the key lesson: large gross flood loss, small net recovery. No MW to normalize.

source_name: South Alexander Solar v. Markel — Hurricane Ida flood/BI claim
solar_flood_use: Second open-dollar solar flood loss with an explicit BI sublimit, from Hurricane Ida.
has_open_numbers: $1,099,614.02 physical damage claim plus a $210,000 business-interruption limit (Hurricane Ida).
does_not_have_open: Project MW capacity; TIV; ground-up distribution.
notes: Corroborates litigation as the most reliable open solar flood dollar source; still no MW denominator.

source_name: DEPCOM Power — 92-MW solar flood response case study
solar_flood_use: Rare capacity-tagged (92 MW) solar flood event with avoided-loss and downtime figures.
has_open_numbers: 92-MW site; 10 of 40 PCS units affected; 12–18 in standing water; $2.5M–$6.5M cited as saved (source-internal inconsistency); $1.8M salvage; $4M avoided loss; $400K–$700K revenue exposure; pads raised +3 ft; 6-week vs 22-week recovery.
does_not_have_open: Clean gross loss; TIV; reconciled saved-figure; net-of-deductible loss.
notes: Has MW, but the headline dollar figures are self-inconsistent; use as directional avoided-loss/downtime evidence only.

source_name: New England Solar (Australia) — flood-related BI / delay disclosure
solar_flood_use: One of the few solar flood datapoints that yields a derived $/MW after dividing disclosed BI by capacity.
has_open_numbers: A$30M lost earnings over a 400-MW project → derived A$75,000/MW (business-interruption / delay basis, not physical damage).
does_not_have_open: Physical-damage $/MW; TIV; currency-normalized USD; gross vs net.
notes: Derived $/MW is BI/delay, not physical damage; flag basis clearly and keep in AUD.

source_name: Valdora Solar (15 MW) — zero-damage flood outcome with freeboard
solar_flood_use: Zero-loss lower anchor: a well-elevated solar site took a major flood with no physical damage.
has_open_numbers: 15-MW site; $0 physical damage; ≈ 1.2 m freeboard design; ≈ 13-day precautionary outage.
does_not_have_open: Any positive loss figure (by design); TIV.
notes: Critical lower-bound anchor: elevation/freeboard can drive physical flood loss to zero even in a large event.

source_name: VDE Americas / AXIS — portfolio peril-mix ground-up loss breakdown
solar_flood_use: Only open source giving flood a share of a quantified solar ground-up loss pool, enabling a derived aggregate flood dollar figure.
has_open_numbers: Flood = 0.197% of ≈ $1.5B ground-up solar losses ≈ $3M derived; portfolio 13,000+ asset-years / 92 GW DC / ≈ $100B TIV / all 50 states / 2012–2025.
does_not_have_open: Per-project flood severity; $/MW; flood loss distribution; affected capacity.
notes: Derived ≈ $3M is a portfolio aggregate over 13 years, not a per-event or per-project severity.

source_name: NREL — PV plant flood resilience / hardening guidance
solar_flood_use: Mechanism guidance on which PV subsystems fail under inundation and how elevation mitigates.
has_open_numbers: Qualitative: inverters, combiner boxes, and DC cabling are the flood-vulnerable failure units; foundation scour and washout are secondary mechanisms.
does_not_have_open: AAL, PML, $/MW, depth-damage percentages.
notes: Supports the electrical/BOS failure-unit argument; not financial.

source_name: IEC 60529 — ingress protection (IP) ratings for PV equipment
solar_flood_use: Defines the submersion thresholds (IPX7/IPX8) that determine whether inundated PV equipment survives.
has_open_numbers: IPX7 = 1 m / 30 min immersion; IPX8 = continuous immersion at negotiated depth; junction boxes commonly IP68 (e.g., 1.5 m / 30 min).
does_not_have_open: Dollar losses; damage ratios; solar AAL.
notes: Sets the depth/duration ingress thresholds behind any solar flood depth-damage assumption.

source_name: DOE / FEMP — flood elevation guidance for critical energy equipment
solar_flood_use: Elevation-standard context (100-/500-yr) for siting solar electrical equipment above flood levels.
has_open_numbers: Guidance to elevate critical equipment above the 100-yr and preferably 500-yr flood elevation.
does_not_have_open: AAL, PML, $/MW, claims.
notes: Mechanism/standard; reinforces freeboard as the primary flood mitigation knob.

source_name: FEMA NFIP — national flood insurance claims statistics (generic)
solar_flood_use: Generic (non-solar) flood claim severity and coverage-cap context, downgraded for solar use.
does_not_have_open: Any solar/utility-scale asset; $/MW; solar AAL.
notes: Building/contents flood claims only; generic magnitude context, not a solar benchmark.

source_name: NOAA NCEI — billion-dollar flooding disasters (generic)
solar_flood_use: National flood-event loss scale for macro context; not asset-specific.
has_open_numbers: ≈ 45 billion-dollar flood events totaling ≈ $203.3B (≈ $4.5B average per event).
does_not_have_open: Solar breakouts; $/MW; asset-class losses.
notes: Macro scale only; establishes flood as a major national peril, no solar attribution.

source_name: U.S. Large-Scale Solar Photovoltaic Database
solar_flood_use: Exposure denominator and geolocation for joining solar assets to flood-hazard layers (floodplain overlay).
has_open_numbers: v4.0 / April 2026; 6,611 facilities; ≈ 131,573 MW AC; array boundaries/locations enable floodplain intersection.
does_not_have_open: Losses, claims, insured values, flood elevation per asset.
notes: Required to normalize external flood claims/models and to compute floodplain-exposed capacity.

source_name: EIA-860 annual generator data
solar_flood_use: Generator-level capacity/status crosswalk for flood-exposure reconciliation alongside USPVDB.
has_open_numbers: Generator-level data for plants ≥ 1 MW; 2025 early release posted June 9, 2026.
does_not_have_open: Insurance claims, AAL/PML, flood losses.
notes: Capacity/vintage/owner status; no direct loss numbers.
